title: Kube Play - pass arguments to build
body:
Create a buildah SystemContext from the existing cli arguments
Pass the SystemContext to the build
Add system test

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
No

```release-note
None
```

Resolves: #20890

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan*, *ygalblum* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan,ygalblum] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@edsantiago maybe you help me find what I'm doing wrong here. The test I added always fails when running all the tests (locally or in the CI) and sometimes when running it separately locally. It always fails in the `run_podman login` line: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20966/files#diff-d3acce6fc9725bf464aafb70ec081bf119159d098c06c76204cb18559e2bbfacR947
I copied these lines from other tests that I've never seen failed at this point. Not sure what's wrong here. WDYT?

I stared at it a long time this morning and could not figure it out. I'm on PTO today, but I just stared at it some more, and still can't understand it. 

Could you try TEMPORARILY adding `sleep 2` between `start_registry` and the login? `start_registry` tries hard to make sure that the registry is running, but maybe that code is broken?

I will look at it again Tuesday morning with a fresh brain.

Don't bother with the `sleep`. That's not it. There's a sequencing problem; the test failure only happens on a full bats run. I've tried, unsuccessfully, to reproduce it with a reduced set of tests. No luck. And it's bedtime.

@edsantiago thanks for looking into this.

I was able to reproduce this failure on my machine (Fedora 38) running `./hack/bats --root 700:"private registry"`. The test passes when running with `--rootless`. 

I have tried adding a `sleep 5` between `start_registry` and the `login`, but got the same results:

Having said that, while the failure on my machine is consistent, when I run the test on a Fedora Rawhide VM, the test passes

That's weird. On my F39 laptop, plain `hack/bats 700:private` succeeds, both root and rootless, even in a loop. It only fails if I run `hack/bats` plain, i.e. so it runs the entire test suite. I'm continuing to look into it this morning.
Found it. It may take me a few hours before I can get to working on a fix, but I'll do so tonight.
Fix is in #20998, which is unlikely to merge soon or as-is, but feel free to cherry-pick it so you can at least run your PR through full CI.
Phew. #20998 is ready. If it hasn't merged by the time you start your day, please cherrypick it. And please look at my two comments above.

> Phew. #20998 is ready. If it hasn't merged by the time you start your day, please cherrypick it. And please look at my two comments above.

Thanks a lot. I've rebased and added your comments. I've also added a `podman image pull $IMAGE` at the start to avoid failing when running a single test.

>I've also added a podman image pull $IMAGE

Eeek! No, please no! `setup()` guarantees that this image will be present.Running `pull` is just a recipe for flake disaster.

>assert "Error: short-name resolution enforced but cannot prompt without a TTY

So, I think this is going to be less easy than I'd hoped. That's the message on my laptop. On CI, it's different.

> I can do without checking the error output.

No, what I was trying to say is that it is imperative to check error output, I just don't know how. Maybe `assert "$output" =~ "pattern1|pattern2"`

It is super-critical to check error output because we've had way, way too many instances of
```
   run_podman 125  something-with-a-typo
   [ fails, but not for the expected reason]
```
Or sometimes not a typo, just an unexpected condition.

> I'll try to find time to understand why the image is not being pre-fetched when running a single test

That's because of the missing `rmi $something` that I had you add. TL;DR `setup()` takes pains to remove unwanted images, but in the one-test-only case where it leaves `$IMAGE` _and_ the same image with a different name, it fails. This is not important enough to fix: the code would be much more complicated and unmaintainable than any possible value. Unfortunately, in the development cycle with lots of failed iterations -- like your case right now -- it sucks. And I'm sorry about that.
@edsantiago I've updated the test to check either cases

Test LGTM. Your PR exposed some serious instability in our test framework. Thank you for giving me time to fix it.

(The non-test code, I leave for others to review)
